text: It is well accepted that impairment of cell function or cell loss in hippocampus will interfere with memory processes, since the hippocampus plays a key role in memory (Barker and Warburton, 2011). In Alzheimer's disease, hippocampus and entorhinal cortex are affected early in the disease process and cognitive deficit is correlated with brain atrophy (for review Braskie and Thompson, 2013).
